title: Tetrataenium sublineare
intro: Tetrataenium sublineare is a plant species known for its medicinal properties, commonly used in traditional folk medicine. taxonomy: Tetrataenium sublineare is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apiaceae. It belongs to the genus Tetrataenium, which is a group of plants commonly known as the "four-nerved" plants due to their distinctive leaf venation. The family Apiaceae is a large and diverse group of plants that includes carrots, parsley, and many other familiar vegetables and herbs. The classification of Tetrataenium sublineare reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants in the Apiaceae family. This classification is used by botanists and plant taxonomists to identify and understand the relationships between different plant species.

The plant's leaves and roots are believed to have antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in wound healing and reducing swelling. In some cultures, it is used to treat digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach pain.
